title: Connect External Synths via MIDI
description: Learn to control external synthesizers and drum machines using Circuit Tracks' MIDI output.

steps.title: Gather Cables
steps.instruction: You'll need a 3.5mm TRS to 5-pin DIN MIDI adapter (Type-A) and a standard MIDI cable.
steps.warning: Make sure you have a Type-A adapter. Type-B adapters are wired differently and won't work.

steps.title: Connect MIDI Out
steps.instruction: Connect the 3.5mm end to Circuit Tracks' MIDI Out. Connect the DIN end to your external synth's MIDI In.

steps.title: Set Your Synth's MIDI Channel
steps.instruction: Configure your external synth to receive on MIDI channel 1 or 2 (matching Circuit Tracks' synth tracks).
steps.tip: Check your external synth's manual for how to set MIDI channel.

steps.title: Select Synth Track 1
steps.instruction: Press Synth 1 on Circuit Tracks. This track sends on MIDI channel 1.

steps.title: Play Notes
steps.instruction: Play the pads. You should hear your external synth respond! Notes from Circuit Tracks trigger the external synth.
steps.tip: If you hear both internal and external sounds, use the mixer to mute the internal synth.

steps.title: Sequence Your External Synth
steps.instruction: Program a pattern as normal. The sequencer plays both internal and external synths via MIDI.

outcomes: External synth controlled by Circuit Tracks
Sequenced patterns playing on external gear
